instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
SEO Insertion on Features and Benefits of the Voluntary Retirement Scheme - Article Page
Keyword density and no. of target keywords has been mentioned as comments in the beginning
Suggested additional anchor texts are marked as comments in Yellow. (If required)
Keywords: vrs, voluntary retirement scheme, retirement benefits, retirement calculator
Body Content:
In the present day, the majority of Indians retire from active work life at the age of 60. This is the age of retirement for employees of any organisation. However, there are people in other professions who retire at a later age. Judges, CEOs of banks, and teachers, for instance, can choose to retire at the age of 70. Commonly, most employees and professionals focus on a retirement age that is somewhere in their 60s. Typically, by the age of 65, Indians have reached their retirement.
Among employees and professionals, there are those who opt to back out of employment earlier than the mandated 60 years of age. They seek voluntary retirement. The VRS, or Voluntary Retirement Scheme allows employees to retire before the compulsory retirement age. In addition to this, the VRS provided loyal workers to exit their employment easily if they find themselves in challenging situations.
What Is VRS?
According to the VRS, any employee can opt to retire in advance of the age of retirement, or the date, as the case may be. This is true for both public sector companies and private sector companies. Moreover, there are a few companies that offer VRS to reduce their total workforce. In the most plain terms, the Voluntary Retirement Scheme is called the “Golden Handshake”.
How Does VRS Work?
In any company, if an employee has completed ten years of active service, or has reached the age of 40, he or she is eligible to choose the VRS. All workers and executives of companies, and authorities belonging to cooperative societies are permitted to select VRS. However, directors of cooperative societies cannot avail of the facility of VRS.
According to company regulations, VRS can be used in a company if it wishes to decrease its overall employee strength. Therefore, if someone chooses early retirement, the company does not replace the employee. PSUs, or Public Sector Undertakings, have to take the permission of the Government of India for approval, prior to offering any employees the facility of VRS. However, private companies and firms are allowed to execute their own rules and regulations. Under the laws of the Income Tax Act, Section 2BA, all companies are bound to follow tax rules pertaining to VRS, as laid down by the government. More importantly, an employee who chooses VRS is not permitted to be in employment with another company under the same management.
How Did the Voluntary Retirement Scheme Start in India?
Specific laws that are laid down by the Labour Department of India do not permit employee retrenchment, especially if those employees belong to a labour union. According to the Industrial Disputes Act of 1947, employers are not allowed to reduce the size of their workforce via retrenchment. Such acts are strongly opposed by trade unions of employees. In order for employers to be prevented from making staff redundant, VRS was created as a solution for the problem of retrenchment. As the scheme has a voluntary nature, it is not opposed by trade unions.
When Can a Firm Opt for a Voluntary Retirement Scheme?
All employees are eligible for retirement benefits upon retirement. Therefore, when a firm opts for such a scheme, it is liable to provide benefits according to VRS rules. However, it is important to know when companies opt for VRS. They can do so under particular circumstances only. These special circumstances ensure that employers do not take undue advantage of employees. Frequently, firms implement VRS when they face stiff market competition. Another time when firms tend to opt for VRS is in periods of recession. At times, VRS is offered to employees when a company is in the middle of a merger with another company, or it is being taken over by another firm. Finally, employers may opt for the Voluntary Retirement Scheme when their technology or products become obsolete, and they no longer require the expertise of certain employers.
Features of VRS
The Voluntary Retirement Scheme is a good option for employees depending on the circumstances involved. However, prior to the employee requesting VRS, the employee must be aware of all the features of such a scheme. These are highlighted below:
The employee must have reached the age of above 40 years, or completed ten years of service with the firm.
An employee who retires under the Voluntary Retirement Scheme is eligible to get any dues of provident fund and payments of gratuity from the firm.
The employee is also eligible for any compensation. Any amount that an employee receives up to Rs. 5 lakh, under VRS, is tax-free,  based on Section 10C of the Indian Income Tax Act of 1961. Nonetheless, if an employee wishes to have this benefit, he or she has to file tax returns in the same assessment year as that of the compensation received.
Under the VRS, firms have to offer employees assistance like tax advice. The process of voluntary retirement has to be made as smooth and seamless as possible for the employee.
Employees under a VRS are not allowed to be a part of another company that is under the management of their previous firm.
The firm cannot fill the vacancy caused by an employee under VRS.
What Are the Benefits of VRS?
Retirement benefits can be availed of by an employee under the VRS. However, VRS has benefits for employers too. Here are the benefits of VRS:
Companies are offered an empathetic way to relieve staff of their employment. At the same time, economic efficiency is improved.
The scheme is voluntary and if an employee does opt for it, the process is transparent. Hence, there is no objection from any labour unions.
When the employee retires, all benefits are received. This may help to start a further business or invest funds for retirement requirements.
If a firm offers VRS, it has to comply with the rules and regulations contained in the Industrial Disputes Act of 1947. Thus, employees are granted fair settlements and complete transparency.
When Is VRS Helpful?
A retirement calculator can help you to gauge how much your retirement benefits may be if you opt for VRS. However, you should know which situations VRS is helpful in, and this can ideally be explained with an example:
BSNL and MTNL decided on a merger in 2019. Employees of both companies were offered VRS. All the benefits that were owed were given to VRS employees. Reports stated that around 92,000 opted for VRS.
Conclusion
In case you are compelled to go in for a VRS, you may take the step willingly. However, you may not have thought of retirement before this, and planned for the same. The result? You may find yourself short of funds for your daily living expenses. Given this, you may go through a period of financial strain till you are employed again. In case you do not find gainful employment, your situation may be even worse. Hence, while you are young, you should opt for a retirement plan that helps you during hard times. Life can be uncertain and you cannot depend on VRS benefits alone to keep you financially secure.
input.compliance_comments: [Harshita Rajawat]: Vrs (38) – 3.1%voluntary retirement scheme (7) – 0.5%retirement benefits (3) – 0.2%retirement calculator (1) – 0.08%The Total Keyword Density is 3.88% which is ideal for 1,194 words count article.The placements of the target keywords are apt. (Context: SEO Insertion on Features and Benefits of the Voluntary Retirement Scheme - Arti...)
